id: 1_3mtt_init-schema
namespace: deeptech
description: |
  Creates all database tables and views for the podcast pipeline.
  Run this once manually before the first pipeline run.
  Safe to re-run — all statements use IF NOT EXISTS / CREATE OR REPLACE.

tasks:

  # ===== Core Tables =====

  - id: create_podcast_feeds
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: Feed catalog — the source of truth for which RSS feeds to process.
    sql: |
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS podcast_feeds (
        feed_id         SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
        feed_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
        rss_url         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
        category        TEXT,
        is_active       BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
        added_at        TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
        last_fetched_at TIMESTAMP
      );

  - id: create_podcast_metadata
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: One row per episode. The main fact table.
    sql: |
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS podcast_metadata (
        guid                    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        title                   TEXT,
        link                    TEXT,
        description             TEXT,
        duration                TEXT,
        creator                 TEXT,
        pubDate                 TEXT,
        audio_url               TEXT,
        created_at              TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
        source                  TEXT,
        duration_seconds        INTEGER,
        pub_date                TIMESTAMP,
        episode_number          INTEGER,
        season_number           INTEGER,
        category                TEXT,
        keywords                TEXT,
        description_word_count  INTEGER,
        audio_file_size_bytes   BIGINT,
        explicit                BOOLEAN,
        image_url               TEXT
      );

  - id: alter_podcast_metadata
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: Add new columns to existing tables (safe if columns already exist).
    sql: |
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS duration_seconds        INTEGER;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pub_date                TIMESTAMP;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS episode_number          INTEGER;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS season_number           INTEGER;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS category                TEXT;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS keywords                TEXT;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS description_word_count  INTEGER;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS audio_file_size_bytes   BIGINT;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS explicit                BOOLEAN;
      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS image_url               TEXT;

  - id: create_podcast_downloads
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: Tracks audio file download status per episode.
    sql: |
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS podcast_downloads (
        guid          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        file_path     TEXT,
        status        TEXT,
        downloaded_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
      );

  - id: create_pipeline_run_log
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: Logs each pipeline execution for observability (Stage 5).
    sql: |
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pipeline_run_log (
        run_id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        flow_id             TEXT,
        started_at          TIMESTAMP,
        finished_at         TIMESTAMP,
        status              TEXT,
        feeds_processed     INTEGER,
        episodes_inserted   INTEGER,
        episodes_skipped    INTEGER,
        download_success    INTEGER,
        download_failed     INTEGER,
        error_message       TEXT
      );

  # ===== Analytical Views (Stage 3) =====

  - id: create_view_episodes_clean
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    description: Clean, typed version of raw metadata used as base for all other views.
    sql: |
      CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_episodes_clean AS
      SELECT
        guid,
        title,
        source,
        category,
        creator,
        pub_date,
        DATE_TRUNC('day',  pub_date)  AS pub_day,
        DATE_TRUNC('week', pub_date)  AS pub_week,
        DATE_TRUNC('month',pub_date)  AS pub_month,
        EXTRACT(DOW  FROM pub_date)   AS day_of_week,
        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM pub_date)   AS hour_of_day,
        duration_seconds,
        ROUND(duration_seconds / 60.0, 1) AS duration_minutes,
        description_word_count,
        explicit,
        audio_url IS NOT NULL         AS has_audio,
        audio_file_size_bytes,
        keywords,
        image_url,
        created_at
      FROM podcas

Output:
id: 4_3mtt_download-audio
namespace: deeptech
description: |
  Audio download pipeline: reads episodes with audio URLs that haven't
  been downloaded yet, downloads the MP3 files with retry logic, and
  records results in podcast_downloads. Runs every 6 hours.

concurrency:
  limit: 1
  behavior: QUEUE

tasks:

  - id: start_log
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    sql: |
      INSERT INTO pipeline_run_log (run_id, flow_id, started_at, status)
      VALUES ('{{ execution.id }}', 'download-audio', NOW(), 'running')
      ON CONFLICT (run_id) DO NOTHING;

  - id: fetch_pending_episodes
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Query
    description: Find episodes that have an audio URL but no download record yet.
    sql: |
      SELECT m.guid, m.audio_url, m.title
      FROM podcast_metadata m
      LEFT JOIN podcast_downloads d ON m.guid = d.guid
      WHERE m.audio_url IS NOT NULL
        AND (d.guid IS NULL OR d.status = 'failed')
      ORDER BY m.created_at DESC
      LIMIT 500
    fetchType: STORE

  - id: download_audio
    type: io.kestra.plugin.scripts.python.Script
    inputFiles:
      pending.ion: "{{ outputs.fetch_pending_episodes.uri }}"
    outputFiles:
      - download_results.csv
    script: |
      import json, os, re, time, csv
      import requests

      AUDIO_DIR = "/app/storage/audio"
      os.makedirs(AUDIO_DIR, exist_ok=True)
      MAX_RETRIES = 3

      def sanitize(title):
        safe = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', title or 'unknown')
        safe = re.sub(r'[\s_]+', '_', safe).strip('_')
        return safe[:100]

      # Read ION-format rows from Kestra STORE output
      items = []
      try:
        import amazon.ion.simpleion as ion
        with open("pending.ion", "rb") as f:
          for val in ion.load(f, single_value=False):
            items.append(dict(val))
      except ImportError:
        # Fallback: parse line-delimited ION as text
        with open("pending.ion", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
          for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if line:
              try:
                row = json.loads(line.replace("'", '"'))
                items.append(row)
              except:
                pass

      print(f"Episodes to download: {len(items)}")
      results = []

      for item in items:
        guid = str(item.get("guid",""))
        url = str(item.get("audio_url",""))
        title = str(item.get("title", guid))
        filename = sanitize(title) + ".mp3"
        filepath = os.path.join(AUDIO_DIR, filename)

        if os.path.exists(filepath):
          results.append({"guid": guid, "file_path": filepath, "status": "skipped"})
          continue

        success = False
        for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):
          try:
            resp = requests.get(url, stream=True, timeout=60)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            with open(filepath, "wb") as af:
              for chunk in resp.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
                af.write(chunk)
            results.append({"guid": guid, "file_path": filepath, "status": "success"})
            success = True
            break
          except Exception as e:
            print(f"[RETRY {attempt}/{MAX_RETRIES}] {filename}: {e}")
            if attempt < MAX_RETRIES:
              time.sleep(2)

        if not success:
          results.append({"guid": guid, "file_path": "", "status": "failed"})

      with open("download_results.csv", "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as rf:
        w = csv.DictWriter(rf, fieldnames=["guid","file_path","status"])
        w.writeheader()
        w.writerows(results)

      ok = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"]=="success")
      skip = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"]=="skipped")
      fail = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"]=="failed")
      print(f"=== AUDIO DOWNLOAD SUMMARY ===")
      print(f"Downloaded: {ok} | Skipped: {skip} | Failed: {fail}")

  - id: create_downloads_staging
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    sql: |
      DROP TABLE IF EXISTS podcast_downloads_staging;
      CREATE TABLE podcast_downloads_staging (
        guid TEXT, file_path TEXT, status TEXT
      );

  - id: load_downloads_staging
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.CopyIn
    format: CSV
    from: "{{ outputs.download_audio.outputFiles['download_results.csv'] }}"
    table: podcast_downloads_staging
    columns: [guid, file_path, status]

  - id: merge_downloads
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    sql: |
      MERGE INTO podcast_downloads AS PD
      USING podcast_downloads_staging AS PDS ON PD.guid = PDS.guid
      WHEN MATCHED THEN
        UPDATE SET file_path = PDS.file_path, status = PDS.status, downloaded_at = NOW()
      WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
        INSERT (guid, file_path, status, downloaded_at)
        VALUES (PDS.guid, PDS.file_path, PDS.status, NOW());

  - id: update_l
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:46:27Z)**_

The schema file confirms those columns are already part of `CREATE TABLE`, so this `ALTER` block is only for older existing databases. A 10-minute stall on `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` is almost never the column work itself; it’s usually PostgreSQL waiting on an `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` table lock.

Output:
id: 3_3mtt_ingest-metadata
namespace: deeptech
description: |
  Metadata-only pipeline: fetches RSS feeds from the catalog, extracts
  enriched episode metadata, and loads into Postgres. Runs hourly.

concurrency:
  limit: 1
  behavior: QUEUE

tasks:

  - id: start_log
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
    sql: |
      INSERT INTO pipeline_run_log (run_id, flow_id, started_at, status)
      VALUES ('{{ execution.id }}', 'ingest-metadata', NOW(), 'running')
      ON CONFLICT (run_id) DO NOTHING;

  - id: fetch_active_feeds
    type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Query
    sql: |
      SELECT feed_id, feed_name, rss_url, category
      FROM podcast_feeds
      WHERE is_active = TRUE
      ORDER BY feed_id
    fetchType: FETCH

  - id: process_feeds
    type: io.kestra.plugin.core.flow.EachSequential
    value: "{{ outputs.fetch_active_feeds.rows }}"
    tasks:

      - id: rss_fetch
        type: io.kestra.plugin.scripts.python.Script
        outputFiles:
          - rss_content.xml
        script: |
          import urllib.request
          import sys
          from pathlib import Path
          
          url = "{{ fromJson(taskrun.value).rss_url }}"
          feed_name = "{{ fromJson(taskrun.value).feed_name }}"
          
          try:
              req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
              with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as response:
                  content = response.read()
              
              # Write to output file
              Path('rss_content.xml').write_bytes(content)
              print(f"Successfully fetched RSS from {feed_name}")
              sys.exit(0)
              
          except Exception as e:
              print(f"Failed to fetch {feed_name} from {url}: {str(e)}")
              sys.exit(1)

      - id: check_fetch_success
        type: io.kestra.plugin.core.flow.If
        condition: "{{ taskrun.state == 'SUCCESS' }}"
        then:
          - id: xml_to_ion
            type: io.kestra.plugin.serdes.xml.XmlToIon
            from: "{{ outputs.rss_fetch[taskrun.value].outputFiles['rss_content.xml'] }}"

          - id: ion_to_json
            type: io.kestra.plugin.serdes.json.IonToJson
            from: "{{ outputs.xml_to_ion[taskrun.value].uri }}"

          - id: extract_podcasts
            type: io.kestra.plugin.scripts.python.Script
            inputFiles:
              json_data.json: "{{ outputs.ion_to_json[taskrun.value].uri }}"
            outputFiles:
              - podcast_data.csv
            script: |
              import json, csv, re
              from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime

              def extract_guid(g):
                return g.get("content") if isinstance(g, dict) else g

              def extract_creator(item):
                return item.get("dc:creator") or item.get("author") or item.get("itunes:author")

              def extract_audio_url(item):
                enc = item.get("enclosure")
                return (enc.get("@url") or enc.get("url")) if isinstance(enc, dict) else None

              def parse_duration(s):
                if not s: return None
                try:
                  parts = str(s).strip().split(':')
                  if len(parts)==1: return int(parts[0])
                  elif len(parts)==2: return int(parts[0])*60+int(parts[1])
                  elif len(parts)==3: return int(parts[0])*3600+int(parts[1])*60+int(parts[2])
                except: return None

              def parse_pub_date(s):
                if not s: return None
                try: return parsedate_to_datetime(str(s)).isoformat()
                except: return None

              def word_count(t):
                return len(re.findall(r'\w+', str(t))) if t else 0

              def parse_explicit(s):
                return str(s).strip().lower() in ('yes','true','1','explicit') if s else False

              def parse_file_size(item):
                enc = item.get('enclosure', {})
                if isinstance(enc, dict):
                  try: return int(enc.get('@length') or enc.get('length') or 0) or None
                  except: return None
                return None

              with open("json_data.json","r",encoding="utf-8") as f:
                raw = json.load(f)

              channel = raw.get("rss",{}).get("channel",{})
              items = channel.get("item",[])
              if isinstance(items,dict): items=[items]

              source = "{{ fromJson(taskrun.value).feed_name }}"
              feed_category = "{{ fromJson(taskrun.value).category }}"

              result = [{
                "title": item.get("title"),
                "link": item.get("link"),
                "description": item.get("description"),
                "duration": item.get("itunes:duration"),
                "duration_seconds": parse_duration(item.ge
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

volumes:
  pgadmin_data:
    driver: local
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
  kestra_postgres_data:
    driver: local
  kestra_data:
    driver: local
  kestra_tmp:
    driver: local
  podcast_audio:
    driver: local

services:
  pgdatabase:
    image: postgres:18
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME}
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql

  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:9.14.0
    environment:
      - PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL=${PGADMIN_USER}
      - PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD=${PGADMIN_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - pgadmin_data:/var/lib/pgadmin
    ports:
      - "8085:80"
    depends_on:
      pgdatabase:
        condition: service_started

  kestra_postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    volumes:
      - kestra_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: kestra
      POSTGRES_USER: kestra
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: k3str4
    healthcheck:
      test: [ "CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER}" ]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
    depends_on:
      pgdatabase:
        condition: service_started

  kestra:
    image: kestra/kestra:v1.3.8
    env_file:
      - .env_encoded
    pull_policy: if_not_present
    # Note that this setup with a root user is intended for development purpose.
    # Our base image runs without root, but the Docker Compose implementation needs root to access the Docker socket
    # To run Kestra in a rootless mode in production, see: https://kestra.io/docs/installation/podman-compose
    user: "root"
    command: server standalone
    volumes:
      - kestra_data:/app/storage
      - podcast_audio:/app/storage/audio
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - kestra_tmp:/tmp/kestra-wd
    environment:
      KESTRA_CONFIGURATION: |
        datasources:
          postgres:
            url: jdbc:postgresql://kestra_postgres:5432/kestra
            driverClassName: org.postgresql.Driver
            username: kestra
            password: k3str4
        kestra:
          server:
            basicAuth:
              username: "admin@kestra.io" # it must be a valid email address
              password: Admin1234!
          repository:
            type: postgres
          storage:
            type: local
            local:
              basePath: "/app/storage"
          queue:
            type: postgres
          tasks:
            tmpDir:
              path: /tmp/kestra-wd/tmp
          url: http://localhost:8080/
    ports:
      - "8089:8080"
      - "8081:8081"
    depends_on:
      kestra_postgres:
        condition: service_started

  streamlit:
    build: ./dashboard
    environment:
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME}
      DB_HOST: ${DB_HOST}
      DB_PORT: 5432
    ports:
      - "8501:8501"
    depends_on:
      pgdatabase:
        condition: service_started
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command** `sed -n '100,160p' README.md`</summary>
```
Chunk ID: 34ab75
Wall time: 0.0000 seconds
Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 670
Output:

Open http://localhost:8501 — the Podcast Pipeline Monitor shows:

- **📊 Overview** — KPIs, top shows, category breakdown, duration stats
- **🎙️ Shows** — per-feed deep dive with duration trends and episode tables
- **📅 Trends** — weekly volume, publishing heatmap, duration vs. description scatter
- **🔧 Pipeline Health** — download status, data quality scores, run logs

## Services

| Service | Image | Role |
|---------|-------|------|
| `pgdatabase` | postgres:18 | Stores podcast metadata, downloads, run logs |
| `kestra_postgres` | postgres:18 | Kestra's internal state database |
| `kestra` | kestra/kestra:v1.3.8 | Orchestrates the RSS → extract → load → download pipeline |
| `pgadmin` | dpage/pgadmin4:9.14.0 | Web-based PostgreSQL admin |
| `streamlit` | Custom (Python 3.11) | Interactive analytics dashboard |

## Kestra Flows

| Flow | Schedule | Purpose |
|------|----------|---------|
| `init-schema` | Manual (once) | Creates all tables and views |
| `seed-feeds` | Manual (once) | Populates the feed catalog |
| `ingest-metadata` | Every hour | Fetches RSS → extracts metadata → loads into Postgres |
| `download-audio` | Every 6 hours | Downloads MP3 files for episodes with audio URLs |

## Database Schema

### Tables
- **`podcast_feeds`** — Feed catalog (name, URL, category, active status)
- **`podcast_metadata`** — Episode data (title, duration, pub date, category, 19 columns)
- **`podcast_downloads`** — Audio download tracking (status, file path, timestamp)
- **`pipeline_run_log`** — Execution logs (start/end time, counts, errors)

### Analytical Views
- **`v_episodes_clean`** — Typed/parsed base view for all analytics
- **`v_feed_stats`** — One summary row per feed
- **`v_publishing_heatmap`** — Day × hour publishing pattern
- **`v_weekly_volume`** — Weekly episode counts by category
- **`v_download_health`** — Daily download status breakdown
- **`v_data_quality`** — Metadata completeness scores per feed

## Project Structure

```
podcast-downloader-pipeline/
├── dashboard/
│   ├── app.py                 # Streamlit dashboard (4 tabs, 15+ charts)
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   └── .dockerignore
├── flows/
│   ├── init-schema.yaml       # Table + view creation
│   ├── seed-feeds.yaml        # Feed catalog seeder
│   ├── ingest-metadata.yaml   # RSS fetch → extract → load metadata
│   └── download-audio.yaml    # Audio file downloader
├── deploy/
│   └── README.md              # Azure / Coolify deployment guide
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .env.example
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:48:19Z)**_

That failure points to the existing table being older than the current `CREATE TABLE` definition. `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` leaves old tables untouched, and your `ALTER` block only backfills the newer analytics columns, not earlier columns like `audio_url` and `source`; I’m going to patch the init schema so reruns repair the whole expected table shape.

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:48:19Z)**_

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:48:21Z)**_

I found the exact mismatch: ingestion and the analytics view both expect 19 metadata columns, but the migration block only guarantees 10 of them. I’m expanding that block to cover every column the downstream flows use, so a rerun can heal an already-created table.

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:48:27Z)**_

   - id: alter_podcast_metadata
     type: io.kestra.plugin.jdbc.postgresql.Queries
-    description: Add new columns to existing tables (safe if columns already exist).

     sql: |

+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS link                    TEXT;
+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS description             TEXT;
+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS duration                TEXT;
+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS creator                 TEXT;
+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pubDate                 TEXT;
+      ALTER TABLE podcast_metadata ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS audio_url               TEXT;

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-16 15:48:45Z)**_

The failure is because your existing `podcast_metadata` table is older than the YAML definition.

`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS podcast_metadata (...)` did nothing because the table already existed, and the `alter_podcast_metadata` task only added the newer analytics fields. It did **not** add older required fields like `audio_url`, so the view failed here:

```sql
audio_url IS NOT NULL AS has_audio
```

I patched [flows/1_3mtt_init-schema.yaml](/home/macmkboy/codes/podcast-downloader-pipeline/flows/1_3mtt_init-schema.yaml:53) so `alter_podcast_metadata` now backfills all expected metadata columns, including:

```sql
audio_url TEXT
source TEXT
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
title/link/description/duration/creator/pubDate
```

Next: push/update this flow in Kestra, then rerun `1_3mtt_init-schema`. It should repair the existing table and then the view creation should pass.

The earlier 10-minute “stall” was very likely Postgres waiting for an exclusive table lock on `podcast_metadata`, probably because another flow was reading/writing it at the same time. The schema task finally completed once that lock cleared.
